input.description: Explore unsloth LoRA and save/export patterns
input.prompt:
I'm working on a project that uses unsloth. I need to find all files related to saving and exporting models, and understand how LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) are structured.

Please explore and report on:

1. Search for all files containing "save_model", "save_pretrained", "export", "push_to_hub" method definitions
2. Look for files that deal with LoRA configuration - specifically rank (r), alpha (lora_alpha), target_modules parameters
3. Find any model wrapper classes that unsloth uses (like FastLanguageModel or similar)
4. Look for any existing ONNX export code
5. Check setup.py or pyproject.toml for dependencies that might be relevant

For each file found, provide the file path and relevant code sections with line numbers. I especially want to see the class definitions and method signatures.

content.text:

2. Look for files that deal with LoRA configuration - specifically rank (r), alpha (lora_alpha), target_modules parameters
3. Find any model wrapper classes that unsloth uses (like FastLanguageModel or similar)
4. Look for any existing ONNX export code
5. Check setup.py or pyproject.toml for dependencies that might be relevant

For each file found, provide the file path and relevant code sections with line numbers. I especially want to see the class definitions and method signatures.

17	    _maybe_fake_quantize_activations,
18	    fast_dequantize,

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)

77	def _create_unsloth_optimizer(
78	    model,
79	    optimizer_cls,
80	    optimizer_kwargs,
81	    embedding_lr = 5e-5,
82	):
83	    lr = optimizer_kwargs["lr"]
84	    weight_decay = optimizer_kwargs.get("weight_decay", 0.0)
85	
86	    param_groups = \
87	    {
88	        "non_embeddings" : {},
89	        "embeddings"     : {},
90	    }
91	
92	    for name, param in model.named_parameters():
93	        if not param.requires_grad: continue
94	        if name.endswith("modules_to_save.default.weight"):
95	            partial_name = name[:-len(".modules_to_save.default.weight")]
96	            partial_name = partial_name[partial_name.rfind(".")+1:]
97	            print(f"Unsloth: Setting lr = {embedding_lr:.2e} instead of {lr:.2e} for {partial_name}.")
98	            param_groups["embeddings"]    [name] = param
99	        else:
100	            param_groups["non_embeddings"][name] = param

219

223	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,

228	
229	    # Our functions
230	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
231	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
232	):
233	    if token is None: token = get_token()
234	
235	    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
236	    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
237	        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
238	    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()
239

241	        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"

245	
246	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":

77	def _create_unsloth_optimizer(
78	    model,
79	    optimizer_cls,
80	    optimizer_kwargs,
81	    embedding_lr = 5e-5,
82	):
83	    lr = optimizer_kwargs["lr"]
84	    weight_decay = optimizer_kwargs.get("weight_decay", 0.0)
85	
86	    param_groups = \
87	    {
88	        "non_embeddings" : {},
89	        "embeddings"     : {},
90	    }
91	
92	    for name, param in model.named_parameters():
93	        if not param.requires_grad: continue
94	        if name.endswith("modules_to_save.default.weight"):
95	            partial_name = name[:-len(".modules_to_save.default.weight")]
96	            partial_name = partial_name[partial_name.rfind(".")+1:]
97	            print(f"Unsloth: Setting lr = {embedding_lr:.2e} instead of {lr:.2e} for {partial_name}.")
98	            param_groups["embeddings"]    [name] = param
99	        else:
100	            param_groups["non_embeddings"][name] = param
101	        pass
102	    pass
103	
104	    optimizer_grouped_parameters = [
105	        {
106	            "params"       : list(param_groups["non_embeddings"].values()),
107	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
108	            "lr"           : lr,
109	        },
110	        {
111	            "params"       : list(param_groups["embeddings"].values()),
112	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
113	            "lr"           : embedding_lr,
114	        },
115	    ]
116	    optimizer = optimizer_cls(optimizer_grouped_parameters, **optimizer_kwargs)
117	    return optimizer
118	pass

121	class UnslothTrainer(SFTTrainer):
122	    def create_optimizer(self):
123	        embedding_learning_rate = getattr(self.args, "embedding_learning_rate", None)
124	        if embedding_learning_rate is None: return super().create_optimizer()
125	
126	        if self.optimizer is None:
127	            optimizer_cls, optimizer_kwargs = SFTTrainer.get_optimizer_cls_and_kwargs(self.args)
128	            self.optimizer = _create_unsloth_optimizer(
129	                self.model,
130	                optimizer_cls,
131	                optimizer_kwargs,
132	                embedding_learning_rate,
133	            )
134	        pass
135	        return self.optimizer
136	    pass

139	# From `trl>=0.13.0`, they changed how to pass several params to the trainer
140	# We need to patch to make the transition smooth
141	def _backwards_compatible_trainer(trainer_class, config_class):
142	    original_init = trainer_class.__init__
143	    
144	    @wraps(original_init)
145	    def new_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
146	        # All Trainer tokenizer are now called processing_class
147	        trainer_params = set(inspect.signature(original_init).parameters.keys())
148	
149	        if "processing_class" in trainer_params and "tokenizer" in kwargs:
150	            kwargs["processing_class"] = kwargs.pop("tokenizer")
151	        pass
152	
153	        if ("args" in kwargs) and (Version(trl.__version__) >= Version("0.13.0.dev0")):
154	            training_args = kwargs.pop("args", None)
155	
156	            # Get parameters that Trainer.__init__ actually expects
157	            trainer_params.remove('self')
158	            trainer_params.remove('args')
159	
160	            # Get fields that should be passed to Config init
161	            config_fields = {
162	                field.name: field for field in dataclasses.fields(config_class) 
163	                if field.init
164	            }
165	            
166	            # Create config dict with valid fields from training_args
167	            config_dict = {
168	                name: getattr(training_args, name)
169	                for name in config_fields
170	                if hasattr(training_args, name)
171	            }
172	
173	            # Get parameters that exist in Config but not in TrainingArguments
174	            from transformers import TrainingArguments
175	            moved_params = \
176	                set(inspect.signature(config_class)     .parameters.keys()) - \
177	                set(inspect.signature(TrainingArguments).parameters.keys())
178	            
179	            # Separate kwargs into trainer kwargs and config kwargs
180	            trainer_kwargs = {}
181	            additional_config_kwargs = {}
182	
183	            for key, value in kwargs.items():
184	                if key in trainer_params: trainer_kwargs[key] = value
185	                elif key in moved_params or key in config_fields:
186	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
187	                else:
188	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
189	                pass
190	            pass
191	
192	            # Update config_dict with additional kwargs
193	            config_dict.update(additional_config_kwargs)
194	
195	            # Create Config with all the collected parameters
196	            # Reinitialising config class with parameters (that were none initially but populated on first init)
197	            # causes the 2nd init to fail as there are mutual exclusive checks on pairs of parameters.
198	            # Refer: https://github.com/huggingface/trl/blob/main/trl/trainer/grpo_config.py#L499-L502 for example
199	            # So we only create config class if the previous init was not TrainingArguments
200	            if not isinstance(training_args, TrainingArguments):
201	                config = config_class(**config_dict)
202	            else:
203	                config = training_args
204	
205	            # Reconstruct kwargs for Trainer
206	            kwargs = trainer_kwargs
207	            kwargs["args"] = config
208	        pass
209	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
210	    pass
211	    return new_init
212	pass
213	
214	
215	def _patch_trl_trainer():
216	    import trl
217	    if hasattr(trl, "__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__"): return
218	    if Version(trl.__version__) <= Version("0.11.0"): return
219	
220	    import trl.trainer
221	    trl_classes = dir(trl.trainer)
222	    trl_trainers = set(x[:-len("Trainer")] for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Trainer"))
223	    trl_configs  = set(x[:-len("Config")]  for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Config"))
224	    trl_classes = list(trl_trainers & trl_configs)
225	
226	    for x in trl_classes:
227	        try:    exec(f"trl.{x}Trainer.__init__ = _backwards_compatible_trainer(trl.{x}Trainer, trl.{x}Config)", globals())
228	        except: continue
229	    pass
230	
231	    trl.__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__ = True
232	pass

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!

    dtype: torch.dtype = torch.bfloat16,
):
    return FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(
        model_name=model_name,
        max_seq_length=max_seq_length,
        load_in_4bit=load_in_4bit,
        fast_inference=fast_inference,
        max_lora_rank=max_lora_rank,
        gpu_memory_utilization=gpu_memory_utilization,
        dtype=dtype,
    )


def get_unsloth_peft_model(
    model,
    lora_rank: int,
    target_modules: list[str] = "all-linear",
    use_gradient_checkpointing: str = False,
    random_state: int = 42,
):
    return FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(
        model,
        r=lora_rank,
        target_modules=target_modules,
        lora_alpha=lora_rank,
        use_gradient_checkpointing=use_gradient_checkpointing,
        random_state=random_state,
    )

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive report:

## Unsloth Save/Export and LoRA Structure Analysis Report

I've completed a thorough exploration of the unsloth project. Here's what I found:

---

### 1. **Save/Export Method Definitions**

#### Primary Save Method
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495299063-b8v212/unsloth/save.py`

**Main Functions:**

- **`unsloth_save_model()` (Lines 205-232)**
  - Signature: `def unsloth_save_model(model, tokenizer, save_directory, save_method="lora", push_to_hub=False, token=None, is_main_process=True, state_dict=None, save_function=torch.save, max_shard_size="5GB", safe_serialization=True, variant=None, save_peft_format=True, ...)`
  - Supports three save methods: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"
  - Handles pushing to HuggingFace Hub with commit messages and tags
  - Parameters for memory management: `temporary_location`, `maximum_memory_usage`

- **`_merge_lora()` (Lines 159-187)**
  - Merges LoRA A and B matrices with base weights
  - Math: `W.addmm_(A.t(), B.t(), alpha=s)` where s is the scaling factor
  - Returns merged weight and bias
  - Handles both 4-bit quantized and float precision models

- **`save_lora_to_custom_dir()` (Lines 2129-2140)**
  - Wrapper function for saving only LoRA adapters to custom directory
  - Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="lora"`

- **`patch_saving_functions()` (Lines 2569-2618)**
  - Dynamically patches model's `push_to_hub()` method
  - Injects "unsloth" tag automatically
  - Updates commit messages and descriptions

#### GGUF Export
- **`save_to_gguf()` (Line 952)** - For llama.cpp format conversion
- **`save_to_gguf_generic()` (Line 2276)** - Generic GGUF export

**Key note:** No ONNX export code found in the codebase.

---

### 2. **LoRA Configuration Parameters**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495299063-b8v212/unsloth/models/llama.py`

**`FastLlamaModel.get_peft_model()` (Lines 2266-2285)**

Core LoRA parameters:
- **`r` (Lines 2268)**: LoRA rank (default: 16) - controls the dimensionality of A and B matrices
- **`target_modules` (Lines 2269-2270)**: Which linear layers to apply LoRA to
  - Default: `["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]`
- **`lora_alpha` (Line 2271)**: Scaling factor (default: 16)
- **`lora_dropout` (Line 2272)**: Dropout for regularization (default: 0.0)
- **`bias` (Line 2273)**: Bias handling (default: "none")
- **`use_rslora` (Line 2279)**: RSLoRA variant support
- **`modules_to_save` (Line 2280)**: Additional modules to save (e.g., embeddings, lm_head)
- **`init_lora_weights` (Line 2281)**: Weight initialization flag
- **`loftq_config` (Line 2282)**: Low-Rank Adaptation with Quantization Aware Training

Also supports `max_lora_rank=64` for vLLM inference optimization (Line 132 in loader.py).

---

### 3. **LoRA Adapter Weight Structure**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495299063-b8v212/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`

**`get_lora_parameters_bias()` (Lines 235-257)**

Returns a 6-tuple for each layer:
1. **W** - Base weight matrix
2. **quant_state** - Quantization state (for 4-bit models)
3. **lora_A[adapter].weight** - LoRA A matrix (shape: `[r, input_dim]`)
   - Line 252: `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight`
4. **lora_B[adapter].weight** - LoRA B matrix (shape: `[output_dim, r]`)
   - Line 253: `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight`
5. **scaling[adapter]** - LoRA scaling factor alpha/r
   - Line 254: `proj.scaling[adapter]`
6. **bias** - Base layer bias

**`get_lora_parameters()` (Lines 190-232)**

Returns 5-tuple without bias:
- Identical A, B, and scaling extraction
- Supports QAT (Quantization Aware Training) with optional fake quantization
- Lines 220-223: Applies fake quantizers if present

**Key LoRA Math:**
- Update formula: `output = X @ W.t() + (X @ A.t()) @ B.t() * scaling`
- Line 176 in save.py: `W.addmm_(A.t(), B.t(), alpha=s)`

**Adapter Dictionary Structure:**
- Multiple named adapters possible (default: "default")
- Active adapter accessed via `proj.active_adapters[0]` or `proj.active_adapter[0]`
- Lines 245-247 in utils.py show adapter resolution

---

### 4. **Model Wrapper Classes**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495299063-b8v212/unsloth/models/loader.py`

**`FastLanguageModel` (Line 107)**
- **Type:** Static class inheriting from `FastLlamaModel`
- **Key Methods:**
  - `from_pretrained()` (Lines 109-177): Loads model with LoRA/QLoRA support
  - `get_peft_model()`: Applies LoRA adapters (inherited from FastLlamaModel)

**Key Loading Parameters:**
- `load_in_4bit=True` (QLoRA)
- `load_in_8bit=False` (8-bit LoRA)
- `load_in_16bit=False` (16-bit LoRA)
- `max_seq_length=2048`
- `dtype=None` (auto-detect)
- `device_map="sequential"`
- `use_gradient_checkpointing="unsloth"`

**Other Model Classes:**
- `FastModel`: Vision models and 8-bit/16-bit full finetuning
- `FastLlamaModel`: Base Llama implementation
- `FastMistralModel`, `FastQwen2Model`, `FastQwen3Model`, etc.

---

### 5. **Dependencies (pyproject.toml)**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495299063-b8v212/pyproject.toml`

## 4. LORA ADAPTER STORAGE & ACCESS

### LoRA Configuration Structure:

**File:** `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2598-2617)

LoRA is configured via PEFT's `LoraConfig`:

```python
lora_config = LoraConfig(
    r = r,                          # Rank (default: 16)
    lora_alpha = lora_alpha,        # Scaling factor (default: 16)
    target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                      "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
    lora_dropout = lora_dropout,    # Dropout (default: 0.0)
    bias = "none",                  # Bias configuration
    task_type = TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,
    layers_to_transform = layers_to_transform,
    init_lora_weights = init_lora_weights,
    loftq_config = loftq_config,    # Optional LoftQ initialization
    use_rslora = use_rslora,        # Rank-Stabilized LoRA
    modules_to_save = modules_to_save,  # Extra modules (lm_head, embed_tokens)
)
```

### LoRA Matrix Access:

**File:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-232)

Two key functions extract LoRA parameters:

1. **`get_lora_parameters(proj)`** (line 190)
   - Returns: `(W, W_quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, scaling)`
   - Accesses: `proj.lora_A[adapter]`, `proj.lora_B[adapter]`, `proj.scaling[adapter]`
   - lora_A and lora_B are PyTorch Linear layers from PEFT

2. **`get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)`** (line 235)
   - Same as above but also returns base layer bias

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Handles disabled/merged adapters
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]  # Linear layer with weight shape (r, in_features)
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]  # Linear layer with weight shape (out_features, r)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight                # Shape: (r, in_features)
    B = lora_B_linear.weight                # Shape: (out_features, r)
    
    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],  # Alpha / sqrt(r)
    )
```

### LoRA in Training:

**File:** `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` (lines 28-250)

The `LoRA_MLP` class implements forward/backward for LoRA-adapted MLPs:

```python
class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
    @staticmethod
    def forward(ctx, X,
                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,  # Gate LoRA
                upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,     # Up LoRA
                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,   # Down LoRA
                ...):
        # Forward: output = X @ (W + A @ B * scale)
        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
        h = _forward_function(e, g)
        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
        return i
```

---

```

**File:** `/unsloth/trainer.py` (lines 33-39)

    "UnslothTrainingArguments",
    "UnslothTrainer",

    "_patch_trl_trainer",
    "UnslothVisionDataCollator",

```

### Primary Public API:

1. **Model Loading:**
   ```python
   from unsloth import FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel
   
   model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(
       model_name="unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct",
       max_seq_length=2048,
       load_in_4bit=True,
   )
   ```

2. **LoRA Configuration:**
   ```python
   model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(
       model,
       r=16,
       lora_alpha=16,
       target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj"],
       lora_dropout=0.0,
       bias="none",
   )
   ```

3. **Training:**
   ```python
   from unsloth import UnslothTrainer, UnslothTrainingArguments
   
   trainer = UnslothTrainer(
       model=model,
       args=UnslothTrainingArguments(...),
       ...
   )
   trainer.train()
   ```

4. **Saving:**
   ```python
   # LoRA only
   model.save_pretrained("lora-model")
   model.push_to_hub("username/lora-model")
   
   # Merged models
   model.save_pretrained_merged("merged-model")
   model.push_to_hub_merged("username/merged-model")
   
   # GGUF export
   model.save_pretrained_gguf("model.gguf")
   model.push_to_hub_gguf("username/model-gguf")
   ```

---

## 6. ONNX-RELATED CODE

**Status:** **NO ONNX SUPPORT FOUND**

Grep search across all Python files returned no results for "onnx" or "ONNX". The project does NOT currently have ONNX export functionality.

**Supported Formats:**
- LoRA adapters (PEFT format) - Native HF format
- Merged models (fp16/int4)
- GGUF format (llama.cpp compatible) - Primary export target
- GGML format (legacy llama.cpp)
- TorchAO format (quantized)

## 7. KEY FILES SUMMARY

| File Path | Size | Purpose |
|-----------|------|---------|
| `/unsloth/save.py` | 2,678 lines | Comprehensive save/export implementation |
| `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | 3,036 lines | Llama model + LoRA integration (largest file) |
| `/unsloth/models/loader.py` | 45KB | Model loading and initialization |

| `/unsloth/models/vision.py` | 47KB | Vision model support |
| `/unsloth/models/rl.py` | 45KB | Reinforcement learning support |

---

## 8. KEY TECHNICAL INSIGHTS

### LoRA Integration Points:

1. **Model Patching:** Models are patched at initialization via `patch_model_and_tokenizer()` in `_utils.py`

2. **LoRA Application:** Applied using PEFT's `get_peft_model()` wrapper, not custom implementation

3. **Kernel Optimization:** Custom CUDA kernels in `fast_lora.py` optimize forward/backward passes for LoRA matrices

4. **Quantization:** Supports 4-bit (QLoRA) and 8-bit LoRA with automatic dequantization

5. **Target Modules:** Default targets Q/K/V/O projections and MLP layers (gate/up/down)

6. **Scaling:** LoRA scaling is: `output = base_output + (A @ B @ scale)` where scale = `lora_alpha / sqrt(r)`

### Save Workflow:

1. **LoRA Only:** Calls PEFT's built-in `save_pretrained()` 
2. **Merged 16-bit:** Dequantizes base model, merges LoRA into weights, saves as fp16
3. **Merged 4-bit:** Merges LoRA, re-quantizes to int4 with bitsandbytes
4. **GGUF:** Uses `convert_hf_to_gguf.py` from llama.cpp for conversion

---

This should provide a complete foundation for understanding the unsloth codebase structure and implementing ONNX export functionality or other features!

## 2. MAIN MODEL/TRAINER CLASSES

### Primary Entry Points:

**File:** `/unsloth/models/loader.py`

- **`FastLanguageModel`** (line 107) - Main class for loading language models

- **`FastModel`** (line 526) - Base model class for all model types

- **`FastVisionModel`** (line 995) - Vision model support

**File:** `/unsloth/trainer.py`

- **`UnslothTrainingArguments`** (line 70) - Training configuration
- **`_patch_trl_trainer()`** - Patches TRL trainers for compatibility
- **`unsloth_train()`** - Main training function (line 44)

---

## 3. SAVE/EXPORT METHODS

**File:** `/unsloth/save.py` (2,678 lines)

### Main Save Functions:

| Function | Line | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|
| `unsloth_save_model()` | 205 | Core saving function (supports lora/merged_16bit/merged_4bit) |
| `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` | 1321 | Save with automatic 4bit→16bit conversion |
| `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` | 1364 | Push merged model to HuggingFace Hub |
| `save_to_gguf()` | 952 | Export to GGUF format (llama.cpp compatible) |
| `save_to_gguf_generic()` | 2276 | Generic GGUF export (vision models) |
| `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` | 1768 | Save model as GGUF locally |
| `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` | 1951 | Push GGUF format to Hub |
| `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` | 2129 | Save LoRA adapters to custom directory |
| `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` | 2211 | Convert LoRA to GGML format |
| `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` | 2143 | Push LoRA as GGML to Hub |
| `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` | 2502 | Save with TorchAO quantization |
| `patch_saving_functions()` | 2569 | Dynamically patches model with save methods |

### Save Method Parameters:

```python
unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory,
    save_method = "lora"  # Options: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"
    push_to_hub = False,
    token = None,
    max_shard_size = "5GB",
    safe_serialization = True,
    temporary_location = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    ...
)
```

---

## 4. LORA ADAPTER STORAGE & ACCESS

### LoRA Configuration Structure:

**File:** `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2598-2617)

LoRA is configured via PEFT's `LoraConfig`:

```python
lora_config = LoraConfig(
    r = r,                          # Rank (default: 16)
    lora_alpha = lora_alpha,        # Scaling factor (default: 16)
    target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                      "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
    lora_dropout = lora_dropout,    # Dropout (default: 0.0)
    bias = "none",                  # Bias configuration
    task_type = TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,
    layers_to_transform = layers_to_transform,
    init_lora_weights = init_lora_weights,
    loftq_config = loftq_config,    # Optional LoftQ initialization
    use_rslora = use_rslora,        # Rank-Stabilized LoRA
    modules_to_save = modules_to_save,  # Extra modules (lm_head, embed_tokens)
)
```

### LoRA Matrix Access:

**File:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-232)

Two key functions extract LoRA parameters:

1. **`get_lora_parameters(proj)`** (line 190)
   - Returns: `(W, W_quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, scaling)`
   - Accesses: `proj.lora_A[adapter]`, `proj.lora_B[adapter]`, `proj.scaling[adapter]`
   - lora_A and lora_B are PyTorch Linear layers from PEFT

2. **`get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)`** (line 235)
   - Same as above but also returns base layer bias

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    # Handles disabled/merged adapters
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]  # Linear layer with weight shape (r, in_features)
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]  # Linear layer with weight shape (out_features, r)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight                # Shape: (r, in_features)
    B = lora_B_linear.weight                # Shape: (out_features, r)
    
    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],  # Alpha / sqrt(r)
    )
```

### LoRA in Training:

**File:** `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` (lines 28-250)

The `LoRA_MLP` class implements forward/backward for LoRA-adapted MLPs:

```python
class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
    @staticmethod
    def forward(ctx, X,
                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,  # Gate LoRA
                upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,     # Up LoRA
                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,   # Down LoRA
                ...):
        # Forward: output = X @ (W + A @ B * scale)
        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
        h = _forward_function(e, g)
        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
        return i
```

---

## 5. ENTRY POINTS & PUBLIC API SURFACE

**File:** `/unsloth/__init__.py` (lines 261-269)

```python
from .models import *
from .models import __version__
from .save import *
from .chat_templates import *
from .tokenizer_utils import *

# Patch TRL trainers for backwards compatibility
_patch_trl_trainer()
```

**File:** `/unsloth/save.py` (lines 48-54)

```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

**File:** `/unsloth/trainer.py` (lines 33-39)

```python
__all__ = [
    "UnslothTrainingArguments",
    "UnslothTrainer",
    "unsloth_train",
    "_patch_trl_trainer",
    "UnslothVisionDataCollator",
]
```

### Primary Public API:

1. **Model Loading:**
   ```python
   from unsloth import FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel
   
   model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(
       model_name="unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct",
       max_seq_length=2048,
       load_in_4bit=True,
   )
   ```

2. **LoRA Configuration:**
   ```python
   model = FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model(
       model,
       r=16,
       lora_alpha=16,
       target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj"],
       lora_dropout=0.0,
       bias="none",
   )
   ```

3. **Training:**
   ```python
   from unsloth import UnslothTrainer, UnslothTrainingArguments
   
   trainer = UnslothTrainer(
       model=model,
       args=UnslothTrainingArguments(...),
       ...
   )
   trainer.train()
   ```

4. **Saving:**
   ```python
   # LoRA only
   model.save_pretrained("lora-model")
   model.push_to_hub("username/lora-model")
   
   # Merged models
   model.save_pretrained_merged("merged-model")
   model.push_to_hub_merged("username/merged-model")
   
   # GGUF export
   model.save_pretrained_gguf("model.gguf")
   model.push_to_hub_gguf("username/model-gguf")
   ```

---

## 6. ONNX-RELATED CODE

**Status:** **NO ONNX SUPPORT FOUND**

Grep search across all Python files returned no results for "onnx" or "ONNX". The project does NOT currently have ONNX export functionality.

**Supported Formats:**
- LoRA adapters (PEFT format) - Native HF format
- Merged models (fp16/int4)
- GGUF format (llama.cpp compatible) - Primary export target
- GGML format (legacy llama.cpp)
- TorchAO format (quantized)

---

## 7. KEY FILES SUMMARY

| File Path | Size | Purpose |
|-----------|------|---------|
| `/unsloth/save.py` | 2,678 lines | Comprehensive save/export implementation |
| `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | 3,036 lines | Llama model + LoRA integration (largest file) |
| `/unsloth/models/loader.py` | 45KB | Model loading and initialization |
| `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | Core LoRA kernels for training |
| `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | LoRA parameter extraction utilities |
| `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` | 68KB | Model patching and utilities |
| `/unsloth/models/vision.py` | 47KB | Vision model support |
| `/unsloth/models/rl.py` | 45KB | Reinforcement learning support |

---

## 8. KEY TECHNICAL INSIGHTS

### LoRA Integration Points:

1. **Model Patching:** Models are patched at initialization via `patch_model_and_tokenizer()` in `_utils.py`

2. **LoRA Application:** Applied using PEFT's `get_peft_model()` wrapper, not custom implementation

3. **Kernel Optimization:** Custom CUDA kernels in `fast_lora.py` optimize forward/backward passes for LoRA matrices

4. **Quantization:** Supports 4-bit (QLoRA) and 8-bit LoRA with automatic dequantization

5. **Target Modules:** Default targets Q/K/V/O projections and MLP layers (gate/up/down)

6. **Scaling:** LoRA scaling is: `output = base_output + (A @ B @ scale)` where scale = `lora_alpha / sqrt(r)`

### Save Workflow:

1. **LoRA Only:** Calls PEFT's built-in `save_pretrained()` 
2. **Merged 16-bit:** Dequantizes base model, merges LoRA into weights, saves as fp16
3. **Merged 4-bit:** Merges LoRA, re-quantizes to int4 with bitsandbytes
4. **GGUF:** Uses `convert_hf_to_gguf.py` from llama.cpp for conversion

---

This should provide a complete foundation for understanding the unsloth codebase structure and implementing ONNX export functionality or other features!

torch_mv = torch.mv
torch_matmul = torch.matmul
torch_addmm  = torch.addmm
torch_empty  = torch.empty
torch_float32  = torch.float32
torch_float16  = torch.float16
torch_bfloat16 = torch.bfloat16

def QUANT_STATE(W): return getattr(W, "quant_state", None)

def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
pass


def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass


def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
    return X
pass

    @torch.inference_mode
    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
        # TODO: After adding XPU BNB support, check this function
        if quant_state is None: return W

gc.collect()
pass


def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass

    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!

    )
    return

def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.

        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["quantization_method"]
    del arguments["first_conversion"]

    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)

    # Non blocking install GGUF first
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):

        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()

# Corrected function to save LoRA to a custom directory
def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:

def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
):
    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint

    Args
      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
    """
    # first merge the lora weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["torchao_config"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
    from torchao import quantize_
    if torchao_config is None:
        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
    else:
        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
        arguments["save_directory"],
        device_map = "auto",
        quantization_config = quantization_config,
        **kwargs,
    )

    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"

    if push_to_hub:
        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
    else:
        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
    pass
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass

import json
import os
import shutil
import tempfile
import pytest
import importlib

from unsloth import FastLanguageModel, FastModel

model_to_test = [
    # Text Models
    "unsloth/tinyllama",
    "unsloth/tinyllama-bnb-4bit",
    "unsloth/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct",
    "unsloth/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct-bnb-4bit",
    "unsloth/Phi-4-mini-instruct",
    "unsloth/Phi-4-mini-instruct-bnb-4bit",
    "unsloth/Qwen2.5-0.5B",
    # Vision Models
    "unsloth/gemma-3-4b-it",
    "unsloth/Llama-3.2-11B-Vision-Instruct-bnb-4bit",
    "unsloth/Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct-bnb-4bit"
]

torchao_models = [
    "unsloth/tinyllama",
    "unsloth/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct",
    #"unsloth/Phi-4-mini-instruct",
    #"unsloth/Qwen2.5-0.5B",
    # Skip the -bnb-4bit variants since they're already quantized
]


# Variables
save_file_sizes = {}
save_file_sizes["merged_16bit"] = {}
save_file_sizes["merged_4bit"] = {}
save_file_sizes["torchao"] = {}

tokenizer_files = [
    "tokenizer_config.json",
    "special_tokens_map.json",
]

@pytest.fixture(scope="session", params=model_to_test)
def loaded_model_tokenizer(request):
    model_name = request.param
    print("Loading model and tokenizer...")

    model, tokenizer = FastModel.from_pretrained(
        model_name, # use small model
        max_seq_length=128,
        dtype=None,
        load_in_4bit=True,
    )

    # Apply LoRA
    model = FastModel.get_peft_model(
        model,
        r=16,
        target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj"],
        lora_alpha=16,
        use_gradient_checkpointing="unsloth",
    )

    return model, tokenizer

@pytest.fixture(scope="session", params=torchao_models)
def fp16_model_tokenizer(request):
    """Load model in FP16 for TorchAO quantization"""
    model_name = request.param
    print(f"Loading model in FP16 for TorchAO: {model_name}")

    model, tokenizer = FastModel.from_pretrained(
        model_name,
        max_seq_length=128,
        dtype=None,
        load_in_4bit=False,  # No BnB quantization
    )

content.thinking:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me now design the implementation plan. Here's what I need to understand:

1. **The `export_to_onnx` function** - This needs to:
   - Iterate over all LoRA-adapted layers in the model (those that are `Peft_Linear` or `Peft_Linear4bit` instances)
   - For each layer, extract:
     - The A matrix (shape: `[r, in_features]`)
     - The B matrix (shape: `[out_features, r]`)
     - The scaling factor
   - Export each A and B matrix as a separate ONNX file (or as tensors within a single ONNX model)
   - Save metadata (rank, alpha, target_modules) as a JSON file
   - Accept `opset_version` parameter (default 17)
   - Save to a specified output directory

2. **How to create ONNX files for tensors** - We use `torch.onnx.export()` to export individual A and B matrices.

3. **Integration** - The function needs to be added to `save.py` as a standalone function.

Now I'm thinking through how to structure this. The function should be attached to the model via `patch_saving_functions()` so it's accessible as `model.save_pretrained_onnx`, and I need to add it to `__all__` for proper exports. For the actual ONNX export, since we're dealing with raw weight tensors rather than a full computational graph, I'll create an ONNX model that wraps each A and B matrix as a constant operation, which is a cleaner approach than trying to force raw tensors into ONNX format. each LoRA layer that represents the LoRA computation: `output = (input @ A.T) @ B.T * scaling`. This would be a small ONNX "matmul chain" model per layer.

But wait - the task says "exports LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) to ONNX format" - this implies saving the tensors themselves, not necessarily the full computation.

Another approach: export the A and B matrices as ONNX constant nodes (initializers) within a model that has the LoRA computation graph. This is the most useful form for downstream consumers who want to deploy the adapters.

Let me think about what makes most sense:

**Option 1: One ONNX file with all LoRA weights as constants/initializers**
- Single `.onnx` file containing all A and B matrices as initializers
- Metadata JSON file with rank, alpha, target_modules

**Option 2: One ONNX file per LoRA layer**
- Separate files for each layer's A and B matrices with the layer name in the filename
- A metadata JSON file for the collection

input.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for adding an `export_to_onnx` method to the unsloth library. Here is the full context:

## Background
Unsloth is a library that accelerates LoRA fine-tuning of LLMs. The task is to add an `export_to_onnx` / `save_pretrained_onnx` capability that exports LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) to ONNX format.

## Key files
- `/unsloth/save.py` (2,678 lines) — ALL existing save/export functions live here. This is the ONLY file that needs modification.
- `/unsloth/kernels/__init__.py` — exports `get_lora_parameters` and `get_lora_parameters_bias`
- `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` — defines `get_lora_parameters(proj)` returning `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)`

## Existing patterns to follow
`save.py` has these exports: `save_pretrained_merged`, `save_pretrained_gguf`, `save_pretrained_ggml`, `save_pretrained_torchao`. Each follows a pattern of:
1. A standalone function taking `self` as first arg
2. Attached to the model in `patch_saving_functions()` via `types.MethodType`

```python
# Example pattern from save_pretrained_torchao:
def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(self, save_directory, tokenizer=None, torchao_config=None, ...):
    """docstring"""
    ...

# In patch_saving_functions():
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
```

## Current imports in save.py (line 29)
```python
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias
```
Note: `get_lora_parameters` is NOT currently imported here (but IS available in kernels package).

## PEFT layer types (already imported in save.py, lines 17-19)
```python
from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear
```

## LoRA parameter extraction (from kernels/utils.py lines 190-232)
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    A = proj.lora_A[adapter].weight   # shape: [r, in_features]
    B = proj.lora_B[adapter].weight   # shape: [out_features, r]
    return (W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

## PEFT config access
Models have `model.peft_config` — a dict of adapter configs. For a default adapter:
```python
peft_config = next(iter(model.peft_config.values()))
rank = peft_config.r                     # int
lora_alpha = peft_config.lora_alpha      # int/float
target_modules = peft_config.target_modules  # set or list of strings
```

## patch_saving_functions (lines 2569-2678)
```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False):
    ...
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
    else:
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic, model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic, model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
    return model
```

## __all__ in save.py (lines 48-54)
```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

## Requirements
- Export A and B matrices for each LoRA-adapted layer as ONNX files
- Save to a specified output directory
- Include metadata.json with rank, alpha, target_modules
- Accept opset_version parameter (default 17)
- Expose as `model.save_pretrained_onnx` (matching existing naming patterns)
- Also expose a standalone `export_lora_to_onnx` in `__all__`

## ONNX Export approach
Each LoRA layer should be exported as a tiny module computing the LoRA delta:
  output = (input @ A.T @ B.T) * scaling
This is the standard approach used for LoRA ONNX exports.

Design the complete step-by-step implementation plan. Include exact code for all new functions, exact line numbers for changes, and how to verify the implementation.

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
